name: Rosano 2005 Tadalafil Cardiovascular-Risk Endothelial Function Extract
population_or_context: Men with erectile dysfunction and increased cardiovascular risk.
sample_size: 32 randomized to tadalafil 20 mg every other day or placebo.
methods: Randomized placebo-controlled 4-week trial with follow-up 2 weeks after discontinuation; endpoints included brachial artery flow-mediated dilation, plasma nitrite/nitrate, and endothelin-1.
population_profile: Men with ED and increased cardiovascular risk; not a mostly healthy 30-50 population.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 20 mg every other day for 4 weeks, with assessment at 4 weeks and 2-week follow-up.
indication_context: Experimental endothelial-function trial in ED/increased cardiovascular-risk context.
safety_findings: Abstract does not provide detailed adverse-event rates; safety must be interpreted using the dedicated tadalafil safety baseline.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate: direct tadalafil exposure and vascular markers, but participants had ED and increased cardiovascular risk.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Small sample, short duration, surrogate endothelial endpoints, abstract-level extraction, and no clinical cardiovascular-outcome assessment.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is population and surrogate endpoint rather than agent identity.
